plants: Soft-stemmed and determined, climbing anything it can reach without ever growing rigid enough to stand alone.
Spreads flat across ground and up walls, covering surfaces so gradually you do not notice until it is everywhere.
A thick woody rope hanging from the canopy, having climbed there using the trees around it as ladders.
A ropey stem that coils and climbs, finding a way up no matter what stands in its path.
Heavy as a cable and tough as wood, draped between trunks high above the forest floor.
